demographics.living_situation: lives at home; primary carer is his daughter who lives with him

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I couldn't stop coughing after swallowing, and my legs just gave out on me today. (Speaking is hard — his daughter is helping him type.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A couple of hours ago he had a bad choking fit on his own spit and couldn't stop coughing, and today his legs keep buckling and he nearly went down. His daughter is worried and rang for help.

symptom_narrative.onset: the weakness in his hands and feet has been creeping up over about 3 weeks; it got noticeably worse today with lots of tripping. The choking fit was about 2 hours ago.
symptom_narrative.duration: 3 weeks of gradual weakness, much worse today; the choking episode lasted a while and left him distressed
symptom_narrative.character: his voice sounds wet and gurgly and weak; he keeps a lot of saliva in his mouth and struggles to clear it; new twitching in his arms and tongue over the last day or two; legs feel like they give way
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the choking really frightened him and he says his breathing feels shallow; he's anxious it will happen again
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: hands, feet and legs for the weakness; throat and mouth for the swallowing and saliva; whole-body tiredness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: steadily progressive over weeks, with a sharp step-down today; the choking came on out of the blue while swallowing saliva
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting quietly and resting; the saliva patch and drops his team gave him help a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: swallowing, lying back, and trying to walk or stand on his own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: wet weak voice, drooling/pooling saliva, visible muscle twitching, near-falls, feeling short of breath and anxious about his breathing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had gradually worsening weakness and swallowing trouble as part of his known condition, but today's choking and the leg buckling are the worst yet
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: needs someone to help him walk safely and can no longer prepare his own meals; his daughter is exhausted from caring for him

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: motor neurone disease (he was told it is ALS)
past_medical_history.how_managed: under a neurology/MND team; takes a tablet for it and uses a patch and drops for saliva

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: drops under the tongue for the saliva (atropine), used when needed

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home, cared for by his daughter; the home has narrow doorways that make his walking frame hard to use; thickened fluids are in the house but he hasn't been using them

objective_data_offered.value: 94% on a fingertip oximeter at home
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an uncalibrated home fingertip oximeter; he is anxious and breathing shallowly

objective_data_offered.value: his daughter counted his breathing at around 22 breaths a minute at rest
wet, gurgly, weak-sounding voice; saliva pooling at the corner of his mouth; visible small twitches in his forearm muscles; sitting slumped and tired-looking
daughter says he nearly fell twice today when his legs buckled, and the choking fit earlier left him red-faced and gasping before it settled
narrow doorways the walking frame won't fit through; bottles of thickening powder on the bench, unopened
